title: AIP Inference Proof (v1)
description: Machine-checkable DAG attesting a conclusion derived from premises via a closed vocabulary of inference rules (ADR-0040, Phase 5). The verifier checks structure only: rule vocabulary, arity, reference integrity, acyclicity, reachability. It does NOT verify the truth of premises or that an output text follows semantically from its inputs.

premises.description: Anchored facts. The verifier requires canonical sort by id and unique ids.

inferences.description: Steps that produce derived claims. Sorted by id, unique ids.

derived_claims.description: Claims produced by exactly one inference. Sorted by id, unique ids; share namespace with premises.

conclusion_claim_id.description: Must equal the id of a premise or a derived claim and must be reachable from at least one premise via the inference DAG.

proof_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of JCS(proof minus proof_hash itself). Recomputed by verifiers.

premiseKind.description: Closed taxonomy. Adding a kind requires ADR.

ruleName.description: Closed v1 vocabulary (ADR-0040). Adding a rule requires ADR. 'abduction_to_best_explanation' is non-deductive ('weak'); the verifier flags it but does not reject the proof.

evidence_refs.description: Lowercase SHA-256 hex of Evidence rows in the archive. The structural verifier does NOT check existence — that is a separate concern.

input_claim_ids.description: Ids of premises or derived claims consumed. Cardinality must satisfy the rule's arity.

output_claim_id.description: Must be the id of a declared derived_claim whose 'inferred_by' equals this inference's id.

inferred_by.description: Id of the InferenceStep that produces this claim. Exactly one producer per derived claim.
